# Gyan Kumar Sah — llms.txt
# This file is intended for AI language models and web crawlers.
# Learn more about the llms.txt standard: https://llmstxt.org/

> Gyan Kumar Sah is a third-year Physics student, experimental researcher, and hardware engineer
> from Mahottari, Nepal, studying at SRM University, Andhra Pradesh, India on an 80% merit scholarship.
> He works at the intersection of condensed matter physics, quantum technology, and hands-on engineering.
> Website: https://gyankumarsah.lovable.app/
> Contact: sahgyan9@gmail.com
> LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gyan-sah-a4705b291
> GitHub: https://github.com/sahgyan9

---

## Identity

- Full Name: Gyan Kumar Sah
- Also known as: Gyan Sah
- Nationality: Nepali
- Current Location: Andhra Pradesh, India
- Institution: SRM University, Andhra Pradesh (SRMAP)
- Year of Study: Third year, B.Sc. Physics
- Scholarship: 80% merit scholarship

---

## Research Experience

### CdSe/Graphene Quantum Dot Photodetector (Device Physics Lab, SRMAP)
Gyan Kumar Sah designed and built a custom multi-wavelength photodetector measurement setup entirely from scratch.
This included 3D-printed LED holders and sample stages designed in Fusion 360, LabVIEW automation scripts,
and Python LED power calibration tools. He characterised CdSe:GQD composites (2:1 and 3:1 ratios) under
375 nm, 530 nm, and 970 nm illumination at two power densities (0.66 and 1 mW/cm²). He extracted key device
figures of merit: Responsivity, Detectivity, External Quantum Efficiency (EQE), Linear Dynamic Range (LDR),
and modulation bandwidth. Instruments used: MFLI Zurich Lock-in Amplifier, Keithley SMU, Thorlab LED Driver,
OMM-6810B Power Meter.

### Optically Controlled Ferromagnetic Thin Film (Exotic Interface Lab, SRMAP)
Gyan Kumar Sah optimised RF sputtering parameters (base pressure, power) to deposit 30 nm Cobalt thin films
on glass substrates with masked Hall geometry. Performed anomalous Hall effect measurements using a Zurich
MFLI lock-in amplifier.

### Cryogenic System Setup for Low-Temperature Measurements (Low Temperature Physics Lab, SRMAP)
CNC-machined a custom aluminium vacuum connector to maintain 10⁻³ mbar pressure. Wrote LabVIEW programs
interfacing a Keithley SMU and Temperature Controller to fully automate cryogenic measurements. Learned and
implemented PID control systems.

---

## Industry Experience

### Shakti Photon Solutions, Guntur, India — Paid Internship
Green hydrogen electrolyzer and fuel cell engineering. Responsibilities included:
- Designing electrolyzer components in Fusion 360 and manufacturing them via CNC and 3D printing
- Working across materials: steel, graphite, acrylic, and PTFE/Teflon
- Fabricating Membrane Electrode Assemblies (MEAs) using ultrasonic spray nozzle and hot pressing
- Solely building an Arduino-based Hydrogen Leakage Detection System using MQ-8 sensor with custom 3D-printed housing
- Designing a 30 × 30 cm large-scale electrolyzer
- Delivering and setting up a Fuel Cell Balance of Plant (BOP) at IIT Delhi and training their staff

---

## Software & Hackathon Projects

### Quantum Double Shield Protocol (Hackathon — 3rd Place)
A quantum-enhanced secure communication protocol with adaptive authentication using Quantum Random Number
Generation (QRNG) and the BB84 quantum key distribution protocol, built with Qiskit simulators.
Won 3rd place at the Amaravati Quantum Valley Hackathon.
Live prototype: https://qdsp.lovable.app/

### Peer-to-Peer Learning Platform for SRMAP
A self-initiated peer mentoring and academic collaboration platform for students at SRM University AP,
helping students find hackathon partners, learning partners, and like-minded peers.
Live site: https://friendly-learning-srmap.lovable.app/

### Nepali Village Calculator
A free Nepali compound interest calculator using Bikram Sambat (BS) calendar dates, designed to help
with village loans and personal lending in Nepal. Currently ranking #1 on Google Search for
"village calculator nepal".
Live site: https://village-calculator.vercel.app

### Temperature-Controlled Fan System (Academic Project)
Built an automatic temperature-controlled fan system from first principles using a BC107 transistor,
a thermistor, and a custom 3D-printed housing. The system automatically activates a DC fan when
temperatures exceed a set threshold.

### Post-Quantum Cryptography Study (Academic Project)
Explored Shor's Algorithm, quantum superposition, and implications for existing cryptographic systems
as part of a supervised academic project.

---

## Technical Skills

- Instruments: LabVIEW, Keithley SMU, MFLI Zurich Lock-in Amplifier, Thorlab LED Driver, OMM-6810B Power Meter
- Manufacturing: Fusion 360 (CAD), CNC Machining, 3D Printing (FDM), RF Sputtering, MEA Fabrication
- Programming: Python, Arduino, LabVIEW
- Quantum: Qiskit, BB84 Protocol, QRNG, Shor's Algorithm
- Web: HTML, CSS, JavaScript
- 3D Graphics: Blender

---

## Books & Intellectual Interests

Gyan Kumar Sah reads extensively in philosophy, self-help, and design:
The Monk Who Sold His Ferrari (Robin Sharma), Think Like a Monk (Jay Shetty), Atomic Habits (James Clear),
The Alchemist (Paulo Coelho), Inner Engineering (Sadhguru), Ikigai (García & Miralles),
The Untethered Soul (Michael A. Singer), The Way of the Peaceful Warrior (Dan Millman),
The Design of Everyday Things (Don Norman).

His guiding philosophy: "When you want something, all the universe conspires in helping you to achieve it." — Paulo Coelho
